en.title: Archive: Keeping your participants up-to-date
en.description: Answer the 5 W-questions, add images, embed video's, attach pdf's and update your audience with a quick email
en.body:
• Keep your project and timeline descriptions up-do-date

Most importantly, you need to make sure the content of your project is up-to-date. You don’t want a participant to fall in a project with outdated or incorrect information. It needs to be clear what the project is about, for each participant, at all times.

So make sure that you always answer the 5 W questions in your project description. Who, what, why, when, where and how? Who is allowed to participate? What is expected of them? Why was this process set up? When do you start and when does it end? How will the input be processed? Use your project and timeline description to add relevant information, such as pdf’s, images, hyperlinks or and details.

  • Send a quick email to keep your participants informed (not visible to others)

You can easily notify individual users or certain user groups by sending quick emails from the platform to specific user groups. Do that when you add relevant text or an event, or maybe when you change the timeline. User groups can be people from a specific neighbourhood, from a certain age, people that participated on a certain project or people that didn’t leave an idea yet.

en.title: Archive: Customising the About page
en.description: Add images, attachments, pictures and contact details!
en.body:
Your platform is launched with default content on the About page with some general information. The About page is always reachable through the 'About' tab in the navigation bar. Use this page to add:

  • platform wide Q&A's

  • general background information

  • images

  • video's

  • attachment (e.g. platform guidelines)

  • and most importantly...

... don't forget the contact details of your organisation on the About page. You should at least add an e-mail address visitors and users of the platform are allowed to contact if they have any questions on your platform our projects.

You can customise the About page by clicking on "Pages & menu" in the admin panel. Then click on "Edit" next to "About". Don't forget: Click save after you are done editing.

Need help or support? Don’t hesitate to get in touch via support@CitizenLab.co

en.title: Archive: Changing the default language of the platform
en.description: Making sure your platform is in the language you want
en.body:
Custom Languages

First impressions matter. Your platform's default language will be the language that users will be greeted with when they first visit, so it's important to get it right!

To do so, go to Settings > General > Languages and make sure that in the Languages section your chosen default language is the first box on the left in the bar. If you want to change it, simply delete the other languages accordingly so that the language you want appears on the very left.

The language that the user registers their account in automatically becomes their “preferred” language, which specifies the language that the platform is displayed to them in and also impacts on how their ideas and comments are auto-translated, should you have the auto-translate function enabled on your platform.

A user can also change their preferred language anytime by going to their account settings, then scrolling down on their profile to the languages setting.

Automated Translations

The upside of custom languages is that you can fully customize the user's experience on the platform to the language of their choice (provided by you), however, the downside is that you have to manually do these translations. When you want to have automatic translations we offer an automatic translation solution of which you can find more information here: Activating auto-translations for comments and ideas

Related Articles:

  • Activating auto-translations for comments and ideas

  • Adding a language to your platform

Do you have any questions or need help? Contact the support team via the chat bubble on the bottom right corner of your platform

en.title: Archive: Adding a language to your platform
en.description: Simply add as you need and change whenever you want
en.body:
Do you want to make your platform available in more than one language? This article explains how you can easily turn your platform into a multilingual one, adding as many languages as you want.

Platform languages

Adding a language to your platform

To add a language to your platform, go to Settings > General > Languages, where you will find a box like the one below. Simply click on the small "down arrow" on the right of the box to see a list of all the currently available languages.

Note: the language that appears on the furthest left will be the default language of your platform.

Note: This does not mean that the platform will automatically translate your content and posts into the other languages. You will still have to manually translate the titles and description of your projects in the languages you've added, so make sure to only add languages you are able and willing to translate to. By clicking on the buttons right above the fields that can be filled in, you can enter the different languages. The button turns green when something has been added.

Can I provide translations for certain projects only?

Yes, you have the option to publish certain project with descriptions in only one language, and others with multiple translations. When you add a language to your platform, it will require you to provide a manually written translation of your project title in each of the languages you've chosen (all languages must turn green), but not the description.

However, if a user is in another language setting and there is no manual translation of the project description written by the admin, the user will only see the project description in the language you have written it. Therefore, we highly encourage you to publish projects in all the languages as soon as possible to include everyone and ensure fair transparency.

For translating the title, you have the option of:

  1. Copy and pasting the direct title in the original language into the text box for the other languages

  2. Use other translation softwares (e.g. Google Translate, DeepL) for a simple translation

Note: It is currently not possible to hide certain projects in 1 or more languages. To allow for inclusive participation, all projects will always be displayed in all added platform languages.

Automated Translations

The upside of default languages is that you can fully customize the user's experience on the platform to the language of their choice (provided by you), however, the downside is that you have to manually do these translations. When you want to have automatic translations we offer an automatic translation solution of which you can find more information here: Activating auto-translations for comments and ideas

Related Articles:

  • Activating auto-translations for comments and ideas

  • The default language of your platform and how to change it

Do you have any questions or need help? Don't hesitate to send a message to support@govocal.com!

en.title: Archive: If I register and create an account, which of my data will be visible to other people on the platform?
en.description: Only the most basic and necessary profile information will be visible on the platform.
en.body:
Creating and registering your account on the platform is the first step in being able to fully participate on the platform, allowing you to leave comments, interact within projects and submit your input.

Data visible to other users on the platform

When you create your account, you will have your own profile page which can be viewed by other platform users (registered and non-registered). They can only see your name, profile picture (recommended but optional), the date you've joined, and any public comments or submissions you've made - nothing more. On project pages, other users can see the submissions or comments you've made the same way you can see what submissions or comments they've left as well.

If you’d like to disable profile pictures on your platform, please Contact the support team via the chat bubble on the bottom right corner of your platform.
Please be aware that this change will apply to all users, profile pictures will be completely disabled across the entire platform.

Data visible to platform admins

Alternatively, platform admins are the people that manage and run the platform, and therefore also have access to other data provided by you on the platform, for instance in the registration process.

All your personal data are subject to the privacy policy which you agreed to when you first register your account. You can find this at anytime by going the bottom of the page and click on "Privacy Policy".

You can also review and change your cookie settings (the trail of data "crumbs" you leave behind as you interact with the platform), which changes the type of data the platform can collect from you.

our platforms are designed to be GDPR compliant by default. This means analytical and functional cookies are not activated unless the user explicitly consents. therefore by default analytical and functional cookies are not activated.

Related Articles:

  • How can I update and delete my user data?

Do you have further questions or need help? Contact the support team via the chat bubble on the bottom right corner of your platform

en.title: Archive: How can I change and delete my user data?
en.description: Go to Profile > 'My Settings' > Edit > Save > Done!
en.body:
To both update and delete your user data, simply follow the steps below:

  1. Click on your Profile on the top-right corner of the screen, and click on 'My Settings' .

  2. Edit your profile details and data on your profile page. Note that your name and profile picture can be seen by other users on the platform.

  3. Click on the red 'Save changes' button to make sure all the changes are saved. A green text saying "Your profile has been saved" should appear to confirm this.
    ​

  4. At any moment, you can choose to delete any optional data if you do not feel comfortable sharing them anymore. You can also choose to delete your account if you choose to no longer participate on the platform. If you have deleted your old account but wish to re-join later for any reason, you can still make another profile at anytime using the same email and details.

Exception: If ID verification is enabled on the platform by the administrators (to verify your identity), some registration fields can be linked to it and pre-filled, and therefore can not be changed. For example, if we receive your domicile via ID verification, to verify you are a citizen of city X, you won’t be able to change this information.

Related Articles:

  • If I register and create an account, which of my data will be visible to other people on the platform?

Do you have further questions or need help? Send us a message at support@citizenlab.co!

en.title: Archive: Choosing Primary & Secondary Colours
en.description: Define the look of your platform and keep it user friendly
en.body:
Having the right primary & secondary colours on your platform is more important than you might think, for multiple reasons. It reflects and defines your platforms brand & identity, makes the platform welcoming and visually pleasing, and ensures information is easy to read for users.

Which colours appear where on the platform?

You can change the settings of these color options in your admin panel, by going to "Settings" > "Branding". The header overlay can be customised from "Pages & Menu" > "Home", clicking "Edit", and selecting the Hero module.

Which combination of colours should I choose?

The name of the game is contrast and simplicity. Although you may already have a few colours in mind that reflect your organisation's brand and identity, only pick the most relevant and suitable ones.

  • Main/primary colour - This is the defining color of your platform. It should be a shade of either Red, Blue or Green.

  • Secondary colours - It can be either the same as the primary colour OR another colour that is complementary to the primary colour. Using the diagram below, based on your primary colour, you can choose any color that is opposite of it on the colour wheel.

  • For text – pick either black or a dark shade of another colour (e.g dark grey, dark blue, dark red).

  • For the overlay – we recommend keeping it the same colour as your primary colour to build a consistent and visually pleasing look to the homepage.

🚨 Selecting "white" or a very light colour as your primary or secondary colour options can make important call to actions (as buttons) disappear.

Branding possibilities available outside of the admin panel

Our team can do custom modifications to the style of your platform, but you should contact us for this:

  • Adding a custom font from Adobe or a custom URL.

  • Inverting the colours of the navigation bar (if you want a dark/coloured background option).

  • Modifying the title font size & weight for the Hero section of the home page.

Related Articles:

  • Changing the look and feel of your platform to match your organization's visual identity

Do you have further questions or need help? Contact the support team via the chat bubble on the bottom right corner of your platform

en.title: Archive: Can a participant change their user data?
en.description: Participants can change or delete their user data (details) anytime in their profile settings.
en.body:
Yes, participants can change or delete their user data (details) anytime by going to their profile. If users feel uncomfortable sharing certain data, they can choose to delete any optional data, or delete their account if they no longer wish to participate and have their account remain online and public.

However, If ID verification is enabled on your platform (to verify participants' identity), some registration fields can be linked to it and pre-filled, and therefore can not be changed. For example, if you obtain data of a user's domicile via ID verification to verify they are a member of community X, they won’t be able to change this information.

To change their data, they can do so by following the steps below:

  1. Click on your Profile on the top-right corner of the screen, and click on 'My Settings'.

  2. Edit your profile details and data on your profile page. Note that your name and profile picture can be seen by other users on the platform.

  3. Click on the red 'Save changes' button to make sure all the changes are saved. A green text saying "Your profile has been saved" should appear to confirm this.
    ​

Do you have further questions or need help? Send us a message at support@citizenlab.co!

en.title: Archive: Can I export the images and attachments of ideas and input on the platform?
en.description: What data and information is included in the Excel file when exporting the ideas
en.body:
When exporting ideas and input from the platform, the data will be exported into a single Excel spreadsheet. Pictures can not be exported, but attachments can be exported. If you make a "global" idea export (Admin > Project > Ideas > Export > Export all ideas of this project), the download link will be included for each idea in that spreadsheet. You can not “batch download” the attachments, you will need to click on each download link individually.

However, the spreadsheet does include multiple other useful information and data points, such as:

  • Input title

  • Description

  • Author's name, email, user ID

  • Publication Date

  • Input metrics (upvotes, downvotes, comments)

  • Input URL

  • Project & Tags

  • Status

Related Articles:

  • Exporting data from your platform

  • Inviting people to the platform

en.title: Archive: Customising the navigation bar
en.description: Add, remove and customize the name of the pages in the navigation bar
en.body:
Admins can add, remove and customize the name of the pages to suit the community's needs and branding. You can configure the navigation bar to provide guidance in your community engagement and match it to your identity.

To get control of the navigation bar you can go to "Pages & menu" in the admin panel of your platform. You'll find a list of all the default pages.

Adding pages to the navigation bar

You can click "Add page" to create a new page and click on "Add to navbar" to make it appear on the navigation bar. You can also remove the pages from the navigation bar by clicking on "Remove from navbar".

You can create as many pages as you wish, and you can add up to a maximum of 7 pages on the navigation bar to be visible to the public. 'Home' and 'All projects', are locked pages and cannot be removed. All pages except for "Home" can be renamed to match your community's identity.

Linking projects to the navigation bar

You can click "Add project to navbar" to link any project to the navigation bar. You can give it a custom name.

The navigation bar will automatically detect, based on the user's attributes, wether the project linked in the navigation bar should be visible to them. This means the navigation bar will change depending on the visibility of the project and the user seeing it.

🚨 These are the things that you can't add (yet) to the navigation bar:

  • Custom links / external links

  • Link to a folder

en.title: Archive: How can I enable or disable messages from the platform?
en.description: Manage the messages you receive by changing your account settings
en.body:
After you have registered on our platform, you can manage the messages you receive from the platform in your account settings.

Simply click on your name in the top right corner, then click on "My settings" and scroll to the bottom of the page.

Under "Notifications" you'll find 4 sections:

  • "Linked to your own post"

  • "Official messages"

  • "Linked to a post you commented on"

  • "Linked to a post you voted on"

Click on "Expand" on the right of each section to change your preferences for messages that fall within a certain section.

  • "Linked to your own post"

  • "Official messages"

  • "Linked to a post you commented on"

  • "Linked to a post you voted on"

Click on "Expand" on the right of each section to change your preferences for messages that fall within a certain section.

en.title: Archive: Can I edit the Accessibility policy, replace it by our own policy, or link to a separate website?
en.body:
GoVocal is committed to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. Current and relevant accessibility standards are applied in our on-going efforts to improve the participant experience for all who visit and use our platforms.

We have worked together with AnySurfer, the Belgian agency specialized in web accessibility, to make our platforms compliant with the latest international accessibility standards WCAG 2.2 AA (look at the official website to see what the criteria include to be certified in this). This means that any user can use your platform, regardless of ability.

To make sure the information that is shared on this page is correct and compliant with your platform, it is not possible to edit this page through the admin panel of the platform. However there exists a workaround:

To edit your accessibility statement, this can be done with a bit of a work-around. If you create a page in the back-end of your platform under 'Pages & Menu' and copy/paste the content of the current accessibility statement onto this new page. You can then edit and make the changes you want. Once this new page is done you can give the support team the URL of this new page and we can then change the link behind the button in the footer so it will lead to that new custom accessibility page.

en.title: Archive: Enabling Azure SSO
en.description: Steps to follow to activate Azure Active Directory as SSO
en.body:
The option for users to register with email is activated by default on all platforms. Next to registering with a Facebook or Google account you can also activate Azure Active Directory as a SSO.

To activate Azure we need the following information:

  1. A tenant ID (This is the ID of the directory in which Azure ID has been configured).

  2. A Client ID of the Azure application in which Azure AD has been configured sometimes also called ‘Application ID’.

Send this information to support@citizenlab.co with the request to activate Azure.

In addition, what the you need to do:

  • Make sure you Open ID Connect configuration has oauth2AllowImplicitFlow set to true, and oauth2AllowIdTokenImplicitFlow set to true, in your manifest.

  • Configure the reply-url as https://<the.platform.domain>/auth/azureactivedirectory/callback , where <the.platform.domain> is replaced by the domain name of you CitizenLab platform

If you plan to change the domain name in the future (if you haven't done that already), you should include the future domain name as well in the reply URLs.

See also Azure Active Directory app manifest docs, including the oauth2AllowImplicitFlow and the oauth2AllowIdTokenImplicitFlow sections.

If you have any further questions, don't hesitate to reach out to support@citizenlab.co.

en.title: Archive: Customising your platform's font
en.description: Match the font of your platform with the one from your website
en.body:
In the settings of the platform you can edit a lot to match the platform with the colors of your organisation and the website of your organisation. The default font of the platform is Public Sans.

You can also customise your platform’s font. At the moment this is not possible through the Admin panel of the platform yet.
​
If you want to change the font of your platform, make sure to contact the support team via the chat bubble on the bottom right corner of your platform to align on the font you want to use.

You need to send us the name of the font you want to use. By default we use Adobe fonts to implement on the platform, so make sure to check if it available here.

If your desired font is not available in the Adobe fonts library, you can send us a CSS file with the font.

Do you have further questions or need help? contact the support team via the chat bubble on the bottom right corner of your platform

en.title: Archive: Customising your platform's logo
en.body:
To make sure your platform is instantly recognizable by citizens and users you can use your organization's logo. The logo will appear on the left hand side of the navigation bar, as well as at the bottom of the homepage. In this article we will explain how you can add a logo, what the right dimensions and format are and some additional information.

Adding a logo

In the admin panel, click on "Settings" > "Branding" and scroll down to "Logo". Click on the box to upload an image of your organisations logo.

Right dimensions & format

The height of the image you use for you logo should be at least 45 pixels, but preferably larger. It can be much larger as it gets downscaled automatically. Width can be whatever it needs to be to respect the aspect ratio of the logo.

The maximum size of the image you use for the logo is 10 MB.

Recommendations

We advice against using a lot of text in your logo. Because the image scales according to the screen on which the image is viewed.

The logo on the bottom of the homepage cannot be changed because it is linked to the logo that is shown on the left of the navigation bar. You can however add information to the bottom of the homepage where you can also add images. Go to "Pages & menu" > "Edit" next to "Home" > "Edit" next to "Bottom info section".

en.title: Archive: How to create a hidden project with a shareable URL
en.body:
There are 3 ways to have a project hidden from the public:

  • Unlisted Projects: Disable visibility from the public, but allow participation

  • Draft folders: using draft folder to keep projects hidden

  • Preview Link: Disable visibility from the public, allow the project to remain in draft, used for testing purposes

Unlisted Projects

By default a project is set as "Listed". This means it will appear on the platform front office, as a normal project where only the access rights and visibility rights from the project/phase settings will impact the way the project appears to the end-user.

When you "Unlist" a project, it is now no longer visible to the public on the front office, and the only way to access it, is by receiving the URL to the project. There is no other way to navigate towards the project. Therefore it will also no longer be part of the homepage and widgets that include project visibility.
​
An unlisted project will also not be indexed by google and cannot be found by searching on a search engine.

This feature is used to allow Admins and Project Managers to have a project that is only accessible to a target group of users. You can find the toggle to enable or disable listing in the 'General' tab of the project (see image)

Note: You can both list and unlist a project at any time.

Draft folders

If you need to make a folder with projects in it hidden and accessible with a URL the only way is by using this workaround: Keeping the folder in "Draft" and publishing projects within it

  1. Create a folder and add the projects in it.

  2. keep the folder on "draft" but put the project on "publish" so participation is open

  3. Share the URL of the project to the people

Since the folder is on "draft", the projects in it will not appear on the homepage. Therefore you will only be able to know it exists if you have the URL link directly to it. If you keep the access rights of the project is "none", we have now created a situation where only people with a specific URL has access to participate to a project without registration.

Downsides to take into consideration:
﻿1. if you know the title of the project you can still find it in the search project function on the platform

2. Do not add any area tags/ topic tags to the project or residents that follow these tags will recieve email notification that a "new project was published"
﻿3. events associated to this project will show up in the events page

Preview Links

Preview links are used to share projects with other administrators or project managers to share a project, even when it is in draft. This way people can test the project before it's going live. You can find more information on how to set this up in this support article.

en.title: Archive: Creating reports with Report Builder
en.description: Within a few clicks, you can generate an easily shareable report to send to your peers or community.
en.body:
With Go Vocal's Report Builder, you can create clear, visual, and tailored reports that enable you to share your community engagement results with colleagues and your community.

Reports can be generated quickly using your platform’s data. Within a few clicks, you can:

  • Use data from ideation projects and surveys, as well as extra insights about your platform’s visitors, to paint a complete picture of your engagement results

  • Drag & drop your preferred insights to generate a custom report

  • Add comments and structure the order of your chosen information

  • Share your final, customized report as a downloadable PDF or share back with your end-users below a phase as a mobile friendly report.

We have made it incredibly easy to use the Report Builder through a clean drag-and-drop interface. You can use a report template to build your report quickly or create your own report.

Could not display content

Table of Contents

  1. How to enter the Report Builder

  2. Results, Charts, Layout and Content

  3. Use AI in your Report

How to enter the Report Builder

There are two types of reports available: Platform reports and Project reports.

  • Platform reports provide a broader view and allow you to report on data across the entire platform.

  • Project reports are limited to data within a specific project.

Additional data available in platform reports includes:

  • Visitors

  • Registrations

  • Participants

  • Demographic information of participants

  • Total projects published within a selected period

  • Methods used

In addition to offering project-specific insights, project reports come with a built-in front-office view, making it easy to share results with your users in a visually accessible way.

Platform Reports:

Only admins have access to platform reports, as these contain platform-wide data.


To create a platform report:

  1. Go to the ‘Reporting’ tab in the back office.

  2. Click ‘Report builder’.

  3. Click ‘Create a report’.

Note: If you don’t see the Reporting tab, this feature may not be included in your current plan. Please contact your Government Success Manager to learn more about enabling it.

Start by adding a title for your report.
Next, choose one of the following options:

  • Start with a blank page

  • Start with a project template

  • Start with a platform template

Once selected, click ‘Create a report’ to enter the Report Builder.

Start with a blank page

If you choose “Start with a blank page”, you’ll enter the Report Builder with an empty canvas.

From there, you can build your report from scratch. Below, you’ll find instructions on how to:

  • Add content using results and charts

  • Edit and customize the layout of your report

This option is ideal if you want full control over the structure and content of your report.

Start with a project/platform template

If you choose “Start with a project template”, you’ll first need to select the project you want to report on.

After clicking “Create a report”, the Report Builder will open with several pre-filled sections, including:

  • About this report – A short overview including the project title, timeline, and number of participants

  • Summary of the report – A section for writing a brief summary of the report (manually filled)

  • Summary of results – A section for summarizing key outcomes (manually filled)

  • Participants timeline – An overview of the project phases in which registered users could participate

  • Most voted ideas – A chart highlighting the most voted ideas from the project

  • Users by gender – A demographic breakdown of users by gender

  • Users by age – A demographic breakdown of users by age

  • Visitors timeline – A timeline showing when and how visitors accessed the project

Some of these elements will be automatically populated with project data (e.g., charts and timelines), while others will need to be manually completed by the report creator.

Project reports

To access reporting at the project level, your project must include a phase with the “Information” participation method. This phase gives you access to the Report Builder tailored to that specific project.

You can find the reporting feature by going to the relevant phase and clicking on the ‘Report’ tab. From there, click ‘Create a report’ to enter the Report Builder.

Once inside, you can choose to:

  • Start with a blank page

  • Start with a project template

In the Report Builder, use the widgets on the left-hand side to add specific data visualizations or text blocks. You can fully customize the report to highlight key insights or provide written commentary based on your project’s context.

Upon creation, the Phase Report will show under the ‘Report’ tab of your information phase. It will only get published when your phase is active, but if you still want to keep it hidden, you can use the ‘Visible’ toggle to control what your end-users can see.

Here’s a view of the report as shown in the front office of your platform.

Note: Reports created in the Report Builder (Reports tab) cannot be added to reports created within the projects.

Results, Charts, Layout, and Content

On the left side of the Report Builder, you'll see a menu that has 2 tabs: 'Widgets' and 'AI' (depending on your plan). In both of these parts, there are items you can drag and drop to the middle of the screen to use them. The differences between widgets and AI uis explained in detail below.

Adding items

If you want to edit a part of the section you just dragged and dropped in - for instance, to add an image or edit the text - click on the part you want to edit, and the editor will pop up on the right side of the screen.

You can also adjust how the different blocks of a section are arranged; or, if no longer needed, you can delete the block.

How to choose the layout

If you are building your report from a blank page, you start by dragging different columns to the middle of the screen. You can change the alignment of the columns on the right and if you want multiple rows of columns, you can add white space to make sure the alignment of the rows is set how you want it. You can even drag and drop columns within other columns!

Widgets

Widgets are items to choose from to use in your report. You can find them in the left column when you're in the report. You can add basic empty blocks like text fields, images and columns that you need to fill in yourself. You can also add results to specific survey questions, inputs with most reactions and add specific inputs. In the last category you can select a variety of charts bases on registration data or on participation in a specific project.

The section 'About this report' consists of 3 items. At the top, the logo of the platform is displayed. Below that, the title of the report is shown. And finally, the third part lists the title of the report, the period of the project, and the project managers. The title and the text of 'About this report' can be edited when you click on the part you want to change.

The section 'Title' can be used for the title of a new chapter or part within your report. It automatically enlarges the text so it's clear that it's a heading.

The section 'Text' is a frame to add text to your report.

The section 'Image' allows you to add an image.

When you picked one of the these sections, you can still edit them by changing the layout or adding other content. Changing the layout can be done by clicking and holding the section you want to move and dragging it to your preferred spot. If you want to edit a section, you simply click on it and the editor will appear to the right.

Here a list of all possible widgets with a short description of what they mean:

  • Text: gives you a box where you can freely write and edit some text

  • Image: gives you the ability to add an image to the report

  • 2 column: gives you the ability to create 2 columns in your report

  • White space: gives you the ability to add a blank section within your report

  • Embed: gives you the ability to embed for example a video in your report

  • Survey Questions: you can visualize specific survey results in your report

  • Most reacted inputs: you can show the most reacted inputs in your report

  • Input: you can show a specific (manually selected) input in your report

  • Community Monitor Health Score: you can show the health score of your community monitor

  • Visitor timeline: you show the visitors timeline in your report

  • Participants timeline: you show the participants timeline in your report

  • Registration timeline: you show the registration timeline in your report

  • Traffic sources: you can show where users are coming from in your report

  • Demographics: you can show certain demographics in your report

  • Participation: you can show participation in your report

  • Methods used: you can show which methods are used in your report

  • Projects: you can show which projects are on the platform in your report

  • Projects Timeline: you can create a timeline/calendar view of the overview of your projects and filter the projects you wanna showcase

How to add and edit Results and Charts

Once you have dragged your desired number of columns into place, you can start filling them with information. There are a couple of options you can choose from to create content with: 'Results' and 'Charts'.

Results

  • 'Survey results' - this section automatically displays results from an in-house survey.

    • Note: It won't take results from external survey tools

    • Certain pricing plans have access to our data grouping feature, which allows you to show survey data for a question grouped by certain demographic attribute (e.g. gender)

    • Results for quantitative questions will show in the form of aggregate bar charts.

    • To add open-ended responses to short or long text questions, use the AI generated summaries.

  • 'Most reacted ideas' - this section automatically displays the most for voted ideas from a certain project

    • A project needs to be selected from the right side after adding this section to your report

    • You can choose how many ideas you want to show

Charts

There are several charts that you can add to the report. When you have selected a certain chart, the title and attributes of the chart can be selected and edited on the right side.

Note: Pie charts or different chart visualisation option cannot be created in the report. You will need to create pie charts external using excel for example, take images of those charts and then add the in an image field to the report.

Data grouping

Data grouping gives you the opportunity to show survey data for a question grouped by registration fields (gender, location, age, etc) or other survey questions. When you selected the right question you can then choose if you want to group the results to that question based on a registration field or on the answers of another question. You can then choose which registration field or question you want to show.

If you cannot access this option it means it is not included in your price plan

Please note at the moment data grouping feature can only be used on single choice questions of surveys and demographics (and does not yet include grouping by year of birth)

Exporting your report

When your report is finished, you can either share it directly in a "share information" phase. But you can also download it as a PDF and add it as an attachment to the project description.

Data of your votes

Voting doesn’t include first, last name and email, but you you can request it from the support team if you need it. You can contact support via the chat bubble on the bottom right of your screen when you're loggin into the platform.

Use AI in your report

Next to the tab 'Widgets' you can find the tab to use AI in your report. This tab is very useful to get a summary about the results for a specific open question from a survey or to get a summary about the inputs you collected in a phase. If you do not see this tab it means is not included in your price plan.
​
First select the project, then select the right phase. If it is a phase with a survey, select the open question you want to summarize. If the phase is about collecting input, the summary will automatically show. You can drag and drop the summary into your report, by hovering over the paragraph icon at the top left of your summary and dragging it onto the report itself.
The in-line references you see in the AI summary bubble that link the summary to specific inputs this is based on will not show when you drag the summary onto the report itself.

Note that the question will not appear only the AI summary. Therefore you will need to manually add the question using the text box widget on top of the summary.

How to share the report

To share a report, click on 'Print' in the top right corner of the report builder or select it on the right from the list of all the reports that you've created.

The file will be downloaded and added to your download folder on your device. You can share this with whomever you choose.

Remark: it might be that in your browser the settings automatically direct to a printer for printing. Look for the “destination” option, there you will be able to select “save as PDF” instead of a printer, if you want the report digitally.
​

Troubleshooting

The weglot button is on my report

For clients with Weglot enabled, the Weglot language switcher may sometimes appear on reports. If you're experiencing this issue, please contact our support team at support@govocal.com, and we'll resolve it for you.

Need more help or support? Contact our Support Team via the chat bubble.

  • Share your final, customized report as a downloadable PDF or share back with your end-users below a phase as a mobile friendly report.

We have made it incredibly easy to use the Report Builder through a clean drag-and-drop interface. You can use a report template to build your report quickly or create your own report.

Could not display content

Table of Contents

  1. How to enter the Report Builder

  2. Results, Charts, Layout and Content

  3. Use AI in your Report

How to enter the Report Builder

There are two types of reports available: Platform reports and Project reports.

  • Platform reports provide a broader view and allow you to report on data across the entire platform.

  • Project reports are limited to data within a specific project.

Additional data available in platform reports includes:

  • Visitors

  • Registrations

  • Participants

  • Demographic information of participants

  • Total projects published within a selected period

  • Methods used

In addition to offering project-specific insights, project reports come with a built-in front-office view, making it easy to share results with your users in a visually accessible way.

Platform Reports:

Only admins have access to platform reports, as these contain platform-wide data.


To create a platform report:

  1. Go to the ‘Reporting’ tab in the back office.

  2. Click ‘Report builder’.

  3. Click ‘Create a report’.

Note: If you don’t see the Reporting tab, this feature may not be included in your current plan. Please contact your Government Success Manager to learn more about enabling it.

Start by adding a title for your report.
Next, choose one of the following options:

  • Start with a blank page

  • Start with a project template

  • Start with a platform template

Once selected, click ‘Create a report’ to enter the Report Builder.

Start with a blank page

If you choose “Start with a blank page”, you’ll enter the Report Builder with an empty canvas.

From there, you can build your report from scratch. Below, you’ll find instructions on how to:

  • Add content using results and charts

  • Edit and customize the layout of your report

This option is ideal if you want full control over the structure and content of your report.

Start with a project/platform template

If you choose “Start with a project template”, you’ll first need to select the project you want to report on.

After clicking “Create a report”, the Report Builder will open with several pre-filled sections, including:

  • About this report – A short overview including the project title, timeline, and number of participants

  • Summary of the report – A section for writing a brief summary of the report (manually filled)

  • Summary of results – A section for summarizing key outcomes (manually filled)

  • Participants timeline – An overview of the project phases in which registered users could participate

  • Most voted ideas – A chart highlighting the most voted ideas from the project

  • Users by gender – A demographic breakdown of users by gender

  • Users by age – A demographic breakdown of users by age

  • Visitors timeline – A timeline showing when and how visitors accessed the project

Some of these elements will be automatically populated with project data (e.g., charts and timelines), while others will need to be manually completed by the report creator.

Project reports

To access reporting at the project level, your project must include a phase with the “Information” participation method. This phase gives you access to the Report Builder tailored to that specific project.

You can find the reporting feature by going to the relevant phase and clicking on the ‘Report’ tab. From there, click ‘Create a report’ to enter the Report Builder.

Once inside, you can choose to:

  • Start with a blank page

  • Start with a project template

In the Report Builder, use the widgets on the left-hand side to add specific data visualizations or text blocks. You can fully customize the report to highlight key insights or provide written commentary based on your project’s context.

Upon creation, the Phase Report will show under the ‘Report’ tab of your information phase. It will only get published when your phase is active, but if you still want to keep it hidden, you can use the ‘Visible’ toggle to control what your end-users can see.

Here’s a view of the report as shown in the front office of your platform.

Note: Reports created in the Report Builder (Reports tab) cannot be added to reports created within the projects.

Results, Charts, Layout, and Content

On the left side of the Report Builder, you'll see a menu that has 2 tabs: 'Widgets' and 'AI' (depending on your plan). In both of these parts, there are items you can drag and drop to the middle of the screen to use them. The differences between widgets and AI uis explained in detail below.

Adding items

If you want to edit a part of the section you just dragged and dropped in - for instance, to add an image or edit the text - click on the part you want to edit, and the editor will pop up on the right side of the screen.

You can also adjust how the different blocks of a section are arranged; or, if no longer needed, you can delete the block.

How to choose the layout

If you are building your report from a blank page, you start by dragging different columns to the middle of the screen. You can change the alignment of the columns on the right and if you want multiple rows of columns, you can add white space to make sure the alignment of the rows is set how you want it. You can even drag and drop columns within other columns!

Widgets

Widgets are items to choose from to use in your report. You can find them in the left column when you're in the report. You can add basic empty blocks like text fields, images and columns that you need to fill in yourself. You can also add results to specific survey questions, inputs with most reactions and add specific inputs. In the last category you can select a variety of charts bases on registration data or on participation in a specific project.

The section 'About this report' consists of 3 items. At the top, the logo of the platform is displayed. Below that, the title of the report is shown. And finally, the third part lists the title of the report, the period of the project, and the project managers. The title and the text of 'About this report' can be edited when you click on the part you want to change.

The section 'Title' can be used for the title of a new chapter or part within your report. It automatically enlarges the text so it's clear that it's a heading.

The section 'Text' is a frame to add text to your report.

The section 'Image' allows you to add an image.

When you picked one of the these sections, you can still edit them by changing the layout or adding other content. Changing the layout can be done by clicking and holding the section you want to move and dragging it to your preferred spot. If you want to edit a section, you simply click on it and the editor will appear to the right.

Here a list of all possible widgets with a short description of what they mean:

  • Text: gives you a box where you can freely write and edit some text

  • Image: gives you the ability to add an image to the report

  • 2 column: gives you the ability to create 2 columns in your report

  • White space: gives you the ability to add a blank section within your report

  • Embed: gives you the ability to embed for example a video in your report

  • Survey Questions: you can visualize specific survey results in your report

  • Most reacted inputs: you can show the most reacted inputs in your report

  • Input: you can show a specific (manually selected) input in your report

  • Community Monitor Health Score: you can show the health score of your community monitor

  • Visitor timeline: you show the visitors timeline in your report

  • Participants timeline: you show the participants timeline in your report

  • Registration timeline: you show the registration timeline in your report

  • Traffic sources: you can show where users are coming from in your report

  • Demographics: you can show certain demographics in your report

  • Participation: you can show participation in your report

  • Methods used: you can show which methods are used in your report

  • Projects: you can show which projects are on the platform in your report

  • Projects Timeline: you can create a timeline/calendar view of the overview of your projects and filter the projects you wanna showcase

How to add and edit Results and Charts

Once you have dragged your desired number of columns into place, you can start filling them with information. There are a couple of options you can choose from to create content with: 'Results' and 'Charts'.

Results

  • 'Survey results' - this section automatically displays results from an in-house survey.

    • Note: It won't take results from external survey tools

    • Certain pricing plans have access to our data grouping feature, which allows you to show survey data for a question grouped by certain demographic attribute (e.g. gender)

    • Results for quantitative questions will show in the form of aggregate bar charts.

    • To add open-ended responses to short or long text questions, use the AI generated summaries.

  • 'Most reacted ideas' - this section automatically displays the most for voted ideas from a certain project

    • A project needs to be selected from the right side after adding this section to your report

    • You can choose how many ideas you want to show

Charts

There are several charts that you can add to the report. When you have selected a certain chart, the title and attributes of the chart can be selected and edited on the right side.

Note: Pie charts or different chart visualisation option cannot be created in the report. You will need to create pie charts external using excel for example, take images of those charts and then add the in an image field to the report.

Data grouping

Data grouping gives you the opportunity to show survey data for a question grouped by registration fields (gender, location, age, etc) or other survey questions. When you selected the right question you can then choose if you want to group the results to that question based on a registration field or on the answers of another question. You can then choose which registration field or question you want to show.

If you cannot access this option it means it is not included in your price plan

Please note at the moment data grouping feature can only be used on single choice questions of surveys and demographics (and does not yet include grouping by year of birth)

Exporting your report

When your report is finished, you can either share it directly in a "share information" phase. But you can also download it as a PDF and add it as an attachment to the project description.

Data of your votes

Voting doesn’t include first, last name and email, but you you can request it from the support team if you need it. You can contact support via the chat bubble on the bottom right of your screen when you're loggin into the platform.

Use AI in your report

Next to the tab 'Widgets' you can find the tab to use AI in your report. This tab is very useful to get a summary about the results for a specific open question from a survey or to get a summary about the inputs you collected in a phase. If you do not see this tab it means is not included in your price plan.
​
First select the project, then select the right phase. If it is a phase with a survey, select the open question you want to summarize. If the phase is about collecting input, the summary will automatically show. You can drag and drop the summary into your report, by hovering over the paragraph icon at the top left of your summary and dragging it onto the report itself.
The in-line references you see in the AI summary bubble that link the summary to specific inputs this is based on will not show when you drag the summary onto the report itself.

Note that the question will not appear only the AI summary. Therefore you will need to manually add the question using the text box widget on top of the summary.

How to share the report

To share a report, click on 'Print' in the top right corner of the report builder or select it on the right from the list of all the reports that you've created.

The file will be downloaded and added to your download folder on your device. You can share this with whomever you choose.

Remark: it might be that in your browser the settings automatically direct to a printer for printing. Look for the “destination” option, there you will be able to select “save as PDF” instead of a printer, if you want the report digitally.
​

Troubleshooting

The weglot button is on my report

For clients with Weglot enabled, the Weglot language switcher may sometimes appear on reports. If you're experiencing this issue, please contact our support team at support@govocal.com, and we'll resolve it for you.

Need more help or support? Contact our Support Team via the chat bubble.

en.title: Archive: What are the recommended dimensions and sizes of the platform images
en.body:
There are multiple places where you can customize your platform with images (pictures or designed visuals or graphics) to make your platform more engaging and communicate the right look and feel. Here are the exact guidelines per image type to make sure your images crop correctly and don't look blurry.

1. Types of images on your platform

An image can be a simple picture or a designed visual or graphic. These are the different types of images that you can build in your platform and projects:

  • Homepage banner

  • Project/folder card

  • Project/folder banner (named 'header image' in the project settings)

  • Input (idea) card

  • Logo

  • In-text images

  • Proposals

  • Event cards

2. Image sizes

To avoid pixelated images, always make sure your image is heavy enough, i.e. it needs to have a resolution (dpi) of 72dpi at least. Images should be a png or jpg and maximum of 10 MB in size.

* dpi, or dots per inch, is a measure of the resolution of an image. The higher the dot density, the higher the resolution of the image.

3. Image dimensions, ratio and responsiveness

Recommended dimensions are different per image type. It's important to note that images display differently depending on the device you use to visit the platform. See point 4 for display recommendations.
​
This table outlines the suggested dimensions and ratio per image type. The image upload tool helps you with cropping any image to the right ratio at all times.

Image type

Displayed where?

Dimensions

Ratio


​Mobile ratio

Homepage banner

Can be full-width banner or 1 row or column, based on your settings.

1440 x 480 px
​

3:1

May be displayed as approximately 1:1 on mobile phones

Project/folder card

On homepage or in folder to represent a project

1400 × 1050 px
​
​

4:3

Will also show as 4:3 on mobile phones

Project/folder banner

Displayed on the top of your project or project folder page

1800 × 600px
​

Input (idea) card

Displayed on top of the input (idea e.a) or in the input overview


​

Site Logo

Top left in navigation menu


​Minimum height:
​48px

Event Card within a project

Displayed at the bottom of your project pages

Proposal card

Main picture displayed on the list with all other proposals.


​

In-text image

in project/phase descriptions or survey questions

Image titles are mandatory for accessibility reasons

can be resized in the text

Event Card

At the bottom of project pages AND in the Events tab on the navigation bar

1800 × 600px

3:1

Your platform is responsive. This means images display differently based on the screen type (desktop, tablet, mobile phone). We therefore recommend avoid having any text or info on the image and only use decorative images.
​

  • Why do we recommend not having text or info on images?

Not only is this better for accessibility and readability, but any text is likely to get cropped at different browser sizes. Think of your images as providing visual interest rather than critical content - you can use the title and description fields to convey written information and add additional images to your project and folder descriptions if you'd like to provide more information for your platform visitors.
​

4. Image displaying in the homepage banner (+examples)

For the homepage banner you can choose between 3 options: "Full width", "Two rows" or "Fixed Ratio".

You can change the desired layout of your homepage banner.

For any of the3 layout options it's important to pick an image that preferable has no text or info on it.

For every image type, there is a different behavior, to make sure the platform works well on all devices. The "Safe Zone" is the area that is shown at all times.

Full-width banner

The full-width banner gives the best visual effect but resizes & crops the most. Only use this with abstract images.

Two-rows banner

This banner is in particular useful with images that don’t work well with text from the title, subtitle or button. These items will be pushed below the banner.

Fixed-ratio banner

The fixed-ratio banner maintains it's height and most of it's width at all times. This is a good banner type if you want the main area of the image to be visible at all times.

The recommended ratio for the fixed-ratio banner is 3:1. This image is always cropped to a certain ratio to make sure all crucial aspects are on display at all times. If you upload an image that isn't already a 3:1 ratio, you'll be able to drag the image up and down to make sure the important parts show in the fixed area.

5. Image displaying in projects

In a project there are three places where you can add images: Project/folder card, Project/folder banner (named 'header image' in the settings) and an Input (idea) card.

When you upload an image to use as project/folder card or as project/folder banner, you can drag the image and choose which part of the image is always visible. The grid shows what will always be visible and the vague part of the image will be cut off. When you upload an image that is already the right dimensions, you won't be able to drag the image around.

Idea cards will always show in a 4:3 ratio. Here there is no option to drag the image in the grid.

6. Finding a good image

💡 Having trouble finding a good image? Copyright-free image galleries such as Pexels and Unsplash or Burst can often be a good starting point to find nice images with good quality.

7. Attachments

Each attachment can be up to 50 MB in size. There’s no limit to the number of attachments you can add to a project, as long as each file stays within the 50 MB limit.

Note: While you can add many attachments to a single project, uploading a large number of files may significantly slow down the upload process. For optimal performance, we recommend avoiding projects that need to carry a high volume of attachments.

Opening Attached Files

When users click on attached files such as images or PDFs, the files will automatically download to their device instead of opening within the platform or in a new browser tab.

If you want a file (e.g., a PDF or image) to open in a new browser tab rather than downloading, please use the hyperlink feature and insert a direct URL to the online file (e.g., a hosted image or PDF link).

8. Resizing images

While you are able to resize images in the normal test boxes on the platform by clicked and dragging the extremities up and down to resize it, as soon as you are inside the content builder (project description, report, homepage builder...) it is not possible to edit the size of the image that way. The image will follow the size you upload it as.

There is a trick you an use if you need to resize an image inside the content builder. You can use columns within columns to make the image smaller:

You can find more information on how to use the normal text boxes in our platform here


Need more help or support? Contact our Support Team via the chat bubble.
